item.title: 🤝 Merger · VREOD
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Bridgewell Agribusiness for $13.66M in convertible notes; assumes $30.35M debt
item.description:
Acquires Bridgewell Agribusiness for $13.66M in convertible notes; assumes $30.35M debt

Acquired Bridgewell Agribusiness LLC for a closing purchase price of approximately $13.66 million, paid via unsecured subordinated convertible notes convertible into ~22 million subordinate voting shares at $0.62 per share. The company also assumed approximately $30.35 million in existing indebtedness. The acquisition closed on June 5, 2026, contemporaneously with the execution of the purchase agreement.

About: Vireo Growth Inc. operates as a cannabis company that cultivates, manufactures, processes, and distributes medical and adult-use cannabis products in Maryland, Minnesota, Missouri, Nevada, New York, and Utah. It sells cannabis products through a network of retail dispensaries and distributors. The company was formerly known as Goodness Growth Holdings, Inc. and changed its name to Vireo Growth Inc. in July 2024. Vireo Growth Inc.

  • Counterparty: Bridgewell Agribusiness LLC
  • Deal value: $13.66M equity + $30.35M assumed debt
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-06-05
  • Expected Close: 2026-06-05

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · WDC
item.ns0:headline: Exchanges 1.04M Sandisk shares for WDC stock; close June 22
item.description:
Exchanges 1.04M Sandisk shares for WDC stock; close June 22

Entered into exchange agreements to exchange 1,038,681 shares of Sandisk common stock held by it for shares of its own common stock held by institutional investors. The number of WDC shares will be determined by VWAP over June 16-18, 2026, with closing expected June 22, 2026. The transaction reduces WDC's outstanding shares and its stake in Sandisk.

About: Western Digital Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells data storage devices and solutions based on hard disk drive (HDD) technology in the United States, Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. The company offers internal HDDs, data center drives, data center platforms, external drives, portable drives, NAS for home and office, and accessories. It sells its data storage devices and solutions through its computer manufacturers, sales personnel, dealers, distributors, and retailers.

item.title: 📤 Divestiture · GFF
item.ns0:headline: Closes AMES JV sale; $100M cash + $161M loans + 42.78% equity stake
item.description:
Closes AMES JV sale; $100M cash + $161M loans + 42.78% equity stake

On June 9, 2026, Griffon closed the joint venture of its AMES US and Canada businesses with Venanpri Tools (ONCAP/Onex), receiving $100M cash, $90M Tranche A and $71.1M Tranche B second lien loans (10% PIK interest, due Dec 2029), and a 42.78% equity interest in the JV buyer (Merv HoldCo LLC). A Griffon subsidiary funded the loans. The transaction was executed under an amended master transaction agreement updating ministerial terms and the pre-closing reorganization.

About: Griffon Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides home and building, and consumer and professional products in the United States, Europe, Canada, Australia, and internationally. The Home and Building Products segment manufactures and markets residential and sectional commercial garage doors, rolling steel service doors, fire doors, shutters, steel security grilles, and room dividers. This segment also sells garage door openers.

  • Counterparty: Merv HoldCo LLC (Venanpri/ONCAP)

item.title: 🤝 Merger · ADIL
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Azora Therapeutics; Azora holders get 86.9% of pro forma equity; votes pending on Nasdaq approvals
item.description:
Acquires Azora Therapeutics; Azora holders get 86.9% of pro forma equity; votes pending on Nasdaq approvals

Acquired Azora Therapeutics in a reverse merger, issuing 437,474 shares of common and 12,930.617 shares of Series A Preferred (each convertible into 1,000 common) to Azora shareholders. Former Azora equity holders will own ~86.9% of fully diluted shares post-merger and post-financing; pre-merger Adial holders retain ~13.1%. The merger closed immediately; stockholder meetings are required to approve conversion of preferred, exercise of assumed options and warrants, and a potential reverse stock split to maintain Nasdaq listing.

About: Adial Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on the development of therapeutics for the treatment or prevention of addiction and related disorders. The company’s lead product is AD04, a serotonin-3 antagonist, which is in Phase 3 clinical trials for the treatment of alcohol use disorder. It is also involved in the development of drug candidates for non-opioid pain reduction and other diseases and disorders.

  • Counterparty: Azora Therapeutics, Inc.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-06-11
  • Expected Close: Closed June 11, 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · PAFRY
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Emmerson Resources via scheme; ASX listing conditional; vote June 15
item.description:
Acquires Emmerson Resources via scheme; ASX listing conditional; vote June 15

Pan African Resources is acquiring 100% of Emmerson Resources Limited by way of an Australian Court-approved scheme of arrangement. The ASX has issued a conditional admission letter for Pan African to list under code PAF. The scheme meeting is scheduled for June 15, 2026, with implementation expected by July 1, 2026.

About: Pan African Resources PLC engages in the mining, extraction, production, and sale of gold in South Africa. Its flagship projects include the Barberton Mines that consists of three underground mines, including Fairview, Sheba, and Consort located in the Barberton Greenstone Belt; and Elikhulu tailings retreatment plant in Southern Africa. Pan African Resources PLC is headquartered in Johannesburg, South Africa.

  • Counterparty: Emmerson Resources Limited
  • Announced: 2026-03-09
  • Expected Close: 2026-07-01

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Direction=Lost · CPNG
item.ns0:headline: Lost $410M administrative fine from Korean PIPC; will appeal
item.description:
Lost $410M administrative fine from Korean PIPC; will appeal

Coupang's Korean subsidiary Coupang Corp. was fined approximately $410M by the Korean Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC) for a November 2025 data incident ($278M) and data collection violations ($132M). The fines will be recognized in Q2 2026 operating results; Coupang intends to vigorously pursue judicial relief in the Seoul Administrative Court. Formal written decisions are pending, and final amounts may differ.

About: Coupang, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, owns and operates retail business through its mobile applications and internet websites in South Korea and internationally. It operates through Product Commerce and Developing Offerings segments. The Product Commerce segment includes Korean retail and marketplace offerings; Rocket Fresh, a fresh grocery offering; and advertising products.

  • Counterparty: Korean Personal Information Protection Commission
  • Deal value: $410M
  • Announced: 2026-06-10

item.ns0:headline: Faces $125/share cash+stock bid from GameStop; HSR cleared
item.description:
Faces $125/share cash+stock bid from GameStop; HSR cleared

GameStop disclosed it made a non-binding proposal on May 3, 2026 to acquire all outstanding eBay common stock for $125 per share in a combination of cash and GameStop common stock. GameStop already owns 2.48M shares and holds Put/Call Pairs providing economic exposure to another 39M shares; the HSR Act waiting period expired June 3, 2026. The proposal remains subject to negotiation, due diligence, and eBay board response.

About: eBay Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates marketplace platforms that connect buyers and sellers in the United States, the United Kingdom, China, Germany, and internationally. Its marketplace platform includes its online marketplace at ebay.com, off-platform businesses, and the eBay suite of mobile apps. The company’s platforms enable users to list, sell, buy, and pay various products. The company was founded in 1995 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

item.title: 📉 Delisting · Forced · QSI
item.ns0:headline: Warrants removed from Nasdaq listing
item.description:
Warrants removed from Nasdaq listing

Nasdaq filed Form 25-NSE to remove Quantum-Si's warrants from listing and registration effective June 10, 2026. The warrants will likely trade over-the-counter thereafter. Holders should evaluate liquidity impacts.

The company was ordered to pay approximately US$998,000 of Mexico's legal costs. Silver Bull is evaluating annulment options within 120 days and considering strategic alternatives.

The company was formerly known as Metalline Mining Company and changed its name to Silver Bull Resources, Inc. in April 2011. Silver Bull Resources, Inc.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Settled · KPLT
item.ns0:headline: Settles patent infringement suit with Flexshopper; confidential sum
item.description:
Settles patent infringement suit with Flexshopper; confidential sum

Katapult settled a patent infringement lawsuit filed by Flexshopper in September 2024 involving five patents. The settlement grants Katapult and its affiliates (including Aaron's and CCF Holdings) a perpetual, royalty-free license to the patents, and provides a full release from Flexshopper and patent buyer ReadySett. Katapult paid an undisclosed lump sum; the case was dismissed with prejudice on June 8, 2026.

About: Katapult Holdings, Inc. operates a lease-to-own platform for nonprime consumers in the United States. The company’s platform integrates retailers and e-commerce platforms to enable non-prime customers to purchase everyday durable goods. It also offers Katapult Pay, a POS integrations and mobile app that allows consumers to leverage its virtual credit card technology to shop various durable goods merchants featured in app marketplace.

  • Counterparty: Flexshopper, Inc. and ReadySett LLC
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-06-05
  • Expected Close: 2026-06-08

VinFast has agreed to sell its VFTP subsidiary (Vietnam manufacturing operations) to a purchaser group led by Future Investment Research and Development JSC (including CEO Pham Nhat Vuong as minority participant) for approximately VND13,309.6 billion (US$530 million) in cash. Shareholders approved the transfer on May 27, 2026; completion is subject to closing conditions. The transaction follows a planned split that transfers R&D, IP, and sales into a retained subsidiary (VFVN), while VFTP retains manufacturing and existing debt.

  • Counterparty: Future Investment Research and Development JSC / Pham Nhat Vuong
  • Deal value: US$530 million
  • Consideration: cash
  • Announced: 2026-05-12

item.title: 🔎 Strategic Review · Formal Alternatives · COO
item.ns0:headline: Launches strategic review; settles recall litigation for $271.6M
item.description:
Launches strategic review; settles recall litigation for $271.6M

CooperCompanies announced a strategic review and recorded a $271.6M net pre-tax charge to resolve over 95% of claims from a 2023 fertility media recall. The charge consists of $324.1M in accrued liabilities offset by $52.5M expected insurance recoveries, with majority of payments expected in fiscal 2026. item.description:
Fifth Third Bank triggers default rate on credit facility; covenant breaches disclosed; liquidity risk

Disclosed that Fifth Third Bank asserted events of default under its credit agreement, including breaches of the minimum cash covenant ($5M), fixed charge coverage ratio, total funded debt ratio, and failure to pay term loan obligations on April 9, 2026. The lender increased interest to the default rate (2% above margin) and reserved rights to accelerate all obligations. The company stated it does not have sufficient cash to satisfy accelerated payment obligations if the lender exercises its acceleration rights.

Under the share swap agreement, Blue Cloud will issue 160 million equity shares to ConnectM, valued at approximately $30.4 million, and ConnectM expects to recognize a non-cash gain of ~$18.4 million, lifting pro forma stockholders' equity from ~$2.0M to ~$18.8M. Closing is anticipated by Q3 2026.

About: ConnectM Technology Solutions, Inc., a technology company, focuses on advancing the electrification economy by integrating electrified energy assets with its AI-driven technology solutions platform. It provides residential and light commercial buildings, and all-electric original equipment manufacturers with a platform to accelerate the transition to solar and all-electric heating, cooling, and transportation.

  • Counterparty: Blue Cloud Softech Solutions Limited
  • Deal value: $39.6M (implied at signing)
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-05-05
  • Expected Close: Q3 2026

item.title: 🤝 Merger · BANR
item.ns0:headline: Acquires Pacific Financial for 0.2633 BANR shares (~$17.44/PFLC share at announce)
item.description:
Acquires Pacific Financial for 0.2633 BANR shares (~$17.44/PFLC share at announce)

Corporation filed a registration statement on Form S-4 to register shares of its common stock to be issued in its acquisition of Pacific Financial Corporation. Under the merger agreement dated April 30, 2026, each outstanding share of Pacific Financial common stock will be converted into 0.2633 shares of Banner common stock (cash in lieu of fractional shares). Based on Banner's closing price of $66.25 on April 29, 2026, the implied per-share consideration was $17.44 and the aggregate transaction value was approximately $177 million. Current Pacific Financial shareholders will own approximately 7% of the combined company. The merger requires approval by Pacific Financial shareholders at a special meeting (date to be announced) and receipt of regulatory approvals.

About: Banner Corporation operates as the bank holding company for Banner Bank that provide commercial banking and financial products and services to individuals, businesses, and public sector entities in the United States. It accepts various deposit instruments, including interest-bearing and non-interest-bearing checking accounts, money market deposit accounts, regular savings accounts, and certificates of deposit, as well as treasury management services and retirement savings plans.

  • Counterparty: Pacific Financial Corporation
  • Offer price: 0.2633 BANR shares per PFLC share
  • Deal value: $177 million
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2026-04-30

item.ns0:headline: Defaults on $10M note; Helena liquidates $2.8M collateral
item.description:
Defaults on $10M note; Helena liquidates $2.8M collateral

Received a default notice from Helena Global on its $10M senior secured convertible note, claiming $4.5M due and instructing liquidation of Bitcoin and Solana collateral worth ~$2.8M. The company disputes the amount and is evaluating resolution options. Separately, DevvStream signed a binding term sheet for a $6M Series A convertible preferred private placement to fund its investment in Southern Energy and working capital.

About: DevvStream Corp. operates as a carbon credit generation in Canada and the United States. The company invests in projects or companies that involve in carbon credit markets and provides project management. It generates and manages carbon credits by utilizing the blockchain-based platform. The company also invests in green projects that generate renewable energy, improve energy efficiencies, eliminate, or reduce emissions, and sequester carbon directly from the air.

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · Contested Director Election · SEER
item.ns0:headline: Faces proxy contest from Radoff/Torok; vote July 28
item.description:
Faces proxy contest from Radoff/Torok; vote July 28

Is facing a proxy contest from activist stockholders Bradley Radoff and Michael Torok, who have nominated three director candidates for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting on July 28, 2026. Seer's Board unanimously recommends voting FOR its own seven nominees, arguing the activists' proposals undervalue the company and lack relevant expertise. The activists have also made unsolicited acquisition proposals, the latest on May 14, which the Board rejected as significantly undervaluing Seer's cash and growth potential.

About: Seer, Inc., a life sciences company, engages in developing and commercializing products to decode the secrets of the proteome. It develops Proteograph Product Suite, an integrated solution that comprises consumables, an automation instrumentation, and software that allows researchers to conduct proteomic studies in therapeutic and diagnostic research, and clinical trials.

  • Counterparty: Bradley Radoff / Michael Torok
  • Announced: 2026-06-03

item.description:
Acquires EngineRoom for $4.6M cash+earnout; adds ~$8.1M revenue

CXApp Inc.'s wholly owned subsidiary, CXAI Australia, acquired 100% of Virtus Digital Marketing dba EngineRoom Applications for approximately $4.6 million, consisting of 65% cash at close (~$2.99M), 25% performance-based earnout over 24 months (~$1.15M), and 10% escrow (~$460,000). EngineRoom is expected to generate ~$8.1M annual revenue (94% recurring) and ~$1.6M adjusted EBITDA, boosting CXAI's annualized revenue run-rate from ~$4M to >$12M. The transaction signed and closed simultaneously on June 3, 2026; EngineRoom founder Adam Laurie agreed to remain for at least three years.

It offers CXApp, a software-as-a-service platform with native mapping, analytics, on-device positioning, and applications technologies for use in various applications, such as workplace experience, employee engagement, desk and meeting room reservations, workplace analytics, occupancy management, content delivery, corporate communications and notifications, event management, live indoor mapping, wayfinding, and navigat…

item.title: 🤝 Merger · VYNE
item.ns0:headline: Reverse merger with Yarrow Bioscience; ~35.84 VYNE shares per Yarrow share; combined co to trade as YARW
item.description:
Reverse merger with Yarrow Bioscience; ~35.84 VYNE shares per Yarrow share; combined co to trade as YARW

And Yarrow Bioscience Inc. entered into a merger agreement on December 17, 2025 (amended Jan 30, 2026) under which a VYNE subsidiary will merge into Yarrow, with Yarrow surviving as a wholly owned subsidiary and VYNE renamed Yarrow Bioscience. Each outstanding Yarrow share will convert into approximately 35.8441 VYNE shares (estimated as of May 22, 2026, subject to adjustment based on VYNE's net cash at closing). VYNE shareholders will own ~3.0% of the combined company on a fully diluted basis; Yarrow pre-closing financing investors will own ~35.5%; and existing Yarrow holders ~61.5%. The combined company is expected to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol YARW. VYNE shareholders will vote on the issuance of shares and related proposals at a special meeting. A pre-closing cash dividend is also planned for VYNE holders.

About: VYNE Therapeutics Inc., a pharmaceutical company, focuses on developing various therapeutics for the treatment of immuno-inflammatory conditions. The company develops FCD105, a topical combination foam that has completed Phase III clinical trials for the treatment of moderate-to-severe acne vulgaris; and FMX114, a combination of tofacitinib, which is in Phase IIa preclinical trial for the treatment of mild-to-moderate atopic dermatitis.

  • Counterparty: Yarrow Bioscience Inc.
  • Consideration: stock
  • Announced: 2025-12-17

item.title: ⚔️ Proxy Contest · GNK
item.ns0:headline: Rejects Diana's $24.80/share tender; faces proxy contest for board
item.description:
Rejects Diana's $24.80/share tender; faces proxy contest for board

Genco Shipping & Trading's board unanimously rejected Diana Shipping Inc.'s revised unsolicited tender offer of $24.80 per share in cash, calling it inadequate and below NAV ($26.66–$27.10 analyst consensus). The board recommends shareholders not tender and instead vote FOR Genco's six director nominees on the WHITE card, and WITHHOLD on Diana's handpicked slate aiming to take control. The board cites Jefferies and Morgan Stanley fairness opinions upholding the offer's inadequacy.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide. The company owns and operates dry bulk carrier vessels to transports iron ore, coal, grains, steel products, and other dry-bulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

  • Counterparty: Diana Shipping Inc.
  • Offer price: $24.80 per share
  • Consideration: cash

item.description:

Faces a proxy contest from the Radoff-JEC Group (7.8% owner), which is nominating three directors (Howard H. Berman, Joshua S. Horowitz, Luis E. Rinaldini) for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting. The group also submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire Seer for $2.35 per share in cash plus a contingent value right representing 80% of net proceeds from future asset sales, a 39% premium to the unaffected price. item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Won · OTLK
item.ns0:headline: Wins FDA CRL appeal; resubmits ONS-5010 BLA; 60-day PDUFA
item.description:
Wins FDA CRL appeal; resubmits ONS-5010 BLA; 60-day PDUFA

Announced it has resubmitted the Biologics License Application for ONS-5010/LYTENAVA (bevacizumab-vikg) to the FDA for treatment of wet AMD. The resubmission follows a successful appeal of a Complete Response Letter, with the FDA Office of New Drugs concluding substantial evidence of effectiveness has been established and no additional trials are required. The FDA designated the resubmission as Class 1, with a PDUFA decision expected within 60 days of receipt.

About: Outlook Therapeutics, Inc., a late clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, focuses on developing and commercializing monoclonal antibodies for various ophthalmic indications. Its lead product candidate is ONS-5010, an ophthalmic formulation of bevacizumab product candidate that is in Phase-III clinical trial for the treatment of wet age-related macular degeneration and other retina diseases. The transactions aim to protect stakeholder value and position the company for anticipated debt restructuring discussions with CSC Holdings' creditors.

Its brands include Optimum, Suddenlink, Optimum Mobile, Altice Business, News 12 Networks, Cheddar News, a4 Advertising, and i24 News. The company was founded by Patrick Drahi in 2001 and is headquartered in Long Island City, NY.

The court also granted in part the company's motion to strike certain materials. Following the ruling, Perpetua commenced additional critical path construction activities including road upgrades, worker housing, powerline work, and exploration drilling.

About: Perpetua Resources Corp. engages in the mineral exploration activities in the United States. The company primarily explores for gold, silver, and antimony. Perpetua Resources Corp. was incorporated in 2011 and is headquartered in Boise, Idaho.

Its FedEx Ground segment provides day-certain delivery services to businesses and residences.

Announced its subsidiary KLA-iBotics Holdings filed an F-1 registration statement for a proposed IPO of up to 6,250,000 Class A ordinary shares at an estimated price range of $4.00 to $6.00 per share, representing up to $37.5 million in gross proceeds. The IPO is part of Reitar's M&A, Integration and Spin-off (MIS) strategy and the registration statement is not yet effective; terms are preliminary. Reitar will retain voting control of KLA through its ownership of Class B shares (20 votes per share).

About: Reitar Logtech Holdings Limited, through its subsidiaries, provides construction management and engineering design services. It operates in two segments, Asset Management and Professional Consultancy Services; and Construction Management and Engineering Design Services. The company provides construction management and engineering design services for cold storage facilities, automated warehouses, renovated offices, and tailor-made electrical systems.

item.description:
Faces $24.80/share hostile tender offer by Diana; proxy fight for 6 board seats

Is subject to an all-cash tender offer of $24.80 per share by Diana Shipping Inc., representing a 39% premium to the undisturbed price. Diana, already owning 14.4% of Genco, has nominated six independent directors for election at the June 18 annual meeting and urges shareholders to tender shares. The tender offer expires June 26, 2026, and the board has rejected the offer without engagement.

About: Genco Shipping & Trading Limited, together with its subsidiaries, engages in the ocean transportation of dry bulk cargoes worldwide. The company owns and operates dry bulk carrier vessels to transports iron ore, coal, grains, steel products, and other dry-bulk cargoes. It charters its vessels primarily to trading houses, including commodities traders; producers; and government-owned entities.

item.title: ⚖️ Litigation · Lost · OCS
item.ns0:headline: Phase 3 DIAMOND trials miss primary endpoint; discontinues DME program
item.description:
Phase 3 DIAMOND trials miss primary endpoint; discontinues DME program

Oculis announced topline results from its DIAMOND Phase 3 trials of OCS-01 in diabetic macular edema. The primary endpoint of mean change in BCVA at week 52 was not met in both trials; retinal thickness showed substantial reduction but did not translate into visual gain. Company does not plan to pursue FDA filing for OCS-01 in DME and will focus resources on Privosegtor and Licaminlimab programs. Cash position remains strong at $278M as of March 31, 2026.

About: Oculis Holding AG, a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company, develops novel topical treatments for ophthalmic diseases for both back- and front-of-the-eye. The company's lead candidate is OCS-01, a topical dexamethasone formulation, which is in Phase 3 clinical trials for the treatment of diabetic macular edema; OCS-02, a topical biologic candidate that is in Phase 2b clinical trials for the treatment for keratoconjunctivitis sicca, or dry eye disease; and OCS-05, a novel neuroprotective agent…

  • Announced: 2026-05-29

item.description:

Atari entered into an agreement to acquire Hipster Whale, the mobile developer behind Crossy Road (340M downloads), for initial consideration of $29.3M ($26M cash + $3.3M in newly issued Atari shares) plus up to $10M earn-out over 3 years based on performance. Hipster Whale generated $8.28M revenue and $4.63M EBITDA in trailing twelve months. Closing expected in the coming days.

About: Atari SA operates as a multi-platform, interactive entertainment, and licensing products company worldwide. The company provides video games; monetization of video games through multiple channels, multimedia production, and licensing activities; Atari VCS videogame computer system; and Atari Blockchain, which develops and markets non-fungible tokens (NFTs) and develops ecosystem around the Atari Token.

America's Car-Mart announced its Board has retained Houlihan Lokey as financial advisor to evaluate strategic alternatives, including financing, recapitalization, mergers and acquisitions, and other transactions. A Special Committee was formed to oversee the review, chaired by newly appointed independent director Adam Paul. No specific transaction has been announced.

About: America's Car-Mart, Inc., through its subsidiaries, operates as an automotive retailer in the United States. It primarily sells older model used vehicles and provides financing for its customers. As of April 30, 2022, the company operated 154 dealerships in the South-Central United States. The company was founded in 1981 and is based in Rogers, Arkansas.

item.description:
Acquires Pathfinder Digital Assets via Armada Acquisition Corp. II merger; vote pending

Is merging with Armada Acquisition Corp. II (SPAC) and acquiring Pathfinder Digital Assets LLC. Under the Business Combination Agreement dated October 19, 2025, SPAC shareholders receive one Pubco Class A share per SPAC share, and Pathfinder unit holders receive one Pubco Class A share per unit. The transaction values the combined entity at $230M assuming a $10.00 per share price. Concurrent PIPE investments total $214.05M cash plus 600,000 XRP from Advance Funding Subscribers, $10.5M cash plus 200,000 XRP from Delayed Funding Subscribers, and additional XRP contributions from Ripple and the Sponsor. The filing is a proxy statement for the extraordinary general meeting of Armada shareholders to approve the deal.
